title: Your Horoscope This Week: May 10 To 16
text:
Your Horoscope This Week: May 10 To 16

R29 babes, the moon’s in Pisces as the week begins, while the sun moves through Taurus, giving us a nourishing mix of earth and water. This is slow, soulful, creative energy. You may feel more drawn to beauty, music, food, rest, nature, daydreaming, or simply being present without turning every moment into a productivity challenge. Taurus reminds us to be in the body, while Pisces reminds us to keep the spirit open. Together, they create the kind of atmosphere where inspiration arrives softly yet determinedly.

By midweek, the moon enters Aries, and the tone shifts from dreaming to doing. The ideas or feelings that surfaced earlier in the week start asking for movement, even if it’s just one small first step. You don’t need to launch the whole vision at once (especially as we approach the dark of the moon). But you can commit to sending the email, making the call, sketching the outline, clearing your space, and finally saying the thing that’s been stuck in your mental drafts for days. Aries moon energy can be impatient, but used well, it helps you break through hesitation and remind yourself that momentum often begins before confidence fully arrives.

Then on May 16th at 4:01 p.m. EST, the New Moon in Taurus gifts us a grounded reset. This is the first Taurus New Moon in eight years where Uranus is no longer in Taurus, and the first with Uranus in Gemini, so the collective tone feels different. Less like “the ground is shifting under me” and more like “how do I build while staying flexible?” Venus, the ruler of this new moon, is also in Gemini, adding curiosity, conversation, and experimentation to an otherwise earthy lunation. Set intentions around stability, pleasure, money, embodiment, and values, but leave room for those intentions to evolve.

Read your horoscopes for your Sun and Rising signs for the most in-depth forecast.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Aries Sun & Rising:

Aries, the week opens in a quieter register than you’re used to. With the moon in Pisces activating your solitude sector, you may feel more inward, more observant, less interested in being “on.” It’s a good moment to sit with what’s been on your mind without rushing to act on it. Subtle emotions, lingering thoughts, even intuitive nudges have more space to surface when you’re not constantly moving. Then midweek, the energy flips as the moon enters your sign and brings your fire back. Suddenly, that goal you’ve been thinking about feels doable. You might speak up or initiate something you were hesitating on.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, there’s a sense of direction behind your actions. This lands in your money and security sector, so what you begin now has the potential to grow steadily over time. There’s also a deeper awareness of what you’re building toward and why it matters to you long-term. The impulse to chase quick results may still be there, but this moon is asking for consistency over urgency. Build it step by step, keep showing up, and keep refining. Six months from now, the version of you who stayed committed to the process will be the one enjoying the outcome.

Taurus Sun & Rising:

Taurus, the week begins on a soft, social note. With the Moon in Pisces activating your friendship and community sector, you may feel more open, receptive, and in the mood to connect in ways that feel genuine rather than performative. Conversations flow easily, plans feel lighter, and there’s a sense that you don’t have to try so hard to belong. Let yourself enjoy that. Then midweek, as the moon moves into Aries, your energy turns inward. You might feel the need to step back, recharge, or spend time alone processing everything that’s been moving through you.

By the time your annual new moon arrives on the 16th, it’s your moment to begin again, but this time with more clarity about who you are and what you want. This lunation lands in your identity sector, so the intentions you set now are deeply personal. There’s also a noticeable difference in tone compared to past years. Uranus is no longer in your sign, so this new beginning feels more stable, more rooted, even as you remain open to change. Take your time defining what this next version of you looks like, and make sure to set your new moon intentions prior to May 26th. The more honest and intentional you are now, the more aligned everything becomes in the months ahead.

Gemini Sun & Rising:

Gemini, the week begins with your attention on the bigger picture. With the moon in Pisces activating your career and public path, you’re feeling more reflective about where you’re headed and how you’re being seen. There’s a softer lens on your ambitions, less pressure to perform and more curiosity about what actually feels meaningful. Pay attention to what inspires you rather than what impresses others. Then midweek, as the moon enters Aries, your energy shifts outward again. Conversations pick up, plans form quickly, and you might feel pulled to reconnect with friends or step into spaces where your ideas can move and evolve in real time.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, everything quiets down just enough for you to hear yourself clearly. This moon lands in your solitude and inner world, making it less about external moves and more about internal alignment. It’s a chance to reset mentally and emotionally before your season begins. There’s something powerful about choosing rest on purpose, and letting things settle instead of forcing clarity. What you release now, and what you gently commit to behind the scenes, shapes how you show up in a much bigger way over the next six months.

Cancer Sun & Rising:

Cancer, the week begins with your mind drifting a little further than usual. With the moon in Pisces activating your expansion sector, you may feel pulled toward bigger ideas, future plans, or simply a desire to experience something different. There’s a sense of emotional openness that makes it easier to imagine what’s possible without immediately questioning it. Let yourself explore those thoughts without needing to define them right away. Then midweek, as the moon enters Aries, your focus shifts toward your responsibilities and public life. You may feel a renewed push to take action on something you’ve been considering, especially if it connects to your long-term goals.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, the energy softens again, but in a more grounded, intentional way. This lands in your friendship and community sector, making it a powerful moment to set intentions around the people you surround yourself with and the spaces you choose to invest your energy in. View this as your opportunity to build connections that feel supportive and sustainable, not just emotionally fulfilling in the moment. The more honest you are about who aligns with your vision, the easier it becomes to grow alongside the right people over the next six months.

Leo Sun & Rising:

Leo, the week begins in a more introspective, emotionally aware space than you usually prefer. With the Moon in Pisces activating your depth and intimacy sector, you may feel more sensitive to what’s being exchanged in your relationships… There’s a quiet awareness of where your energy is going and whether it’s being reciprocated in a way that feels good to you. Let yourself sit with that without rushing to fix or define anything. Then midweek, as the moon enters Aries, your energy lifts and expands. You’re ready to move, explore, and act on something that feels aligned with your growth. A conversation or even a spontaneous plan can shift your perspective in a meaningful way.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, your focus turns toward your career and long-term direction. This is a grounded moment to set intentions around what you’re building and how you want to be seen. There’s less urgency here and more clarity about what actually lasts. You don’t need to chase visibility right now… you need to define what you want to be known for. The steps you take from here, even if they feel small, create a steady path toward a new lifestyle that reflects your values over the next six months.

Virgo Sun & Rising:

Virgo, this week you’ll notice the cosmos shifting your attention to your relationships, but in a softer, more intuitive way than usual. With the Moon in Pisces activating your partnership sector, you’re more tuned in to the emotional undercurrents in your one-on-one dynamics. You might notice what feels easy, what feels off, and what you’ve been overthinking that actually just needs to be felt. There’s less need to analyze everything and more openness to simply experiencing connection as it is. Then midweek, as the moon enters Aries, the tone shifts. You’re ready to address something directly, whether it’s a conversation, a boundary, or a decision that’s been lingering.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, the energy opens up in a way that feels refreshing and even a little indulgent. This lands in your expansion sector, but not in a “go do everything at once” way… more like “what would feel really good to grow into?” There’s pleasure in learning, planning a trip, or saying yes to an opportunity that expands your world without overwhelming it. Think: good food in a new city, a class that actually excites you, or a conversation that lingers in the best way. This New Moon invites you to build a life that feels rich, not just productive. And when you follow what genuinely lights you up, the next six months unfold with a lot more ease.

Libra Sun & Rising:

Libra, the week begins in a quieter, more internal rhythm. With the Moon in Pisces activating your work and wellness sector, your attention goes to how you’re actually living day to day: your habits, your energy levels, the way your body feels moving through it all. You might notice what needs a little more care, rest, or more intention. Then midweek, as the moon enters Aries, your focus shifts to your relationships. Conversations become more direct, energy picks up, and you may feel ready to address a pain point or dilemma that’s been lurking just beneath the surface.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, the energy deepens, but in a way that feels grounding and even a little indulgent. This lands in your intimacy and shared resources sector, bringing a sense of richness to emotional and physical connection. You’ll find pleasure in slowing down, being present, and allowing yourself to experience closeness without rushing through it. It can also show up in how you handle money or shared responsibilities, choosing stability and comfort over stress. This New Moon invites you to build trust, both with others and with yourself, in a way that feels steady and satisfying over time.

Scorpio Sun & Rising:

Scorpio, the week begins in your favorite language: feeling first, thinking later. With the Moon in Pisces activating your romance, creativity, and inner child sector, your imagination is wide open. You might catch yourself dreaming about what you wish love felt like, what you want to create, or the version of you that feels the most alive. It can get a little idealistic, sure, but there’s something beautiful about letting yourself go there without immediately shutting it down.

Then midweek, the moon enters Aries and brings you back into your body. Suddenly it’s less about the dream and more about how you support it. Hydration, sleep, movement, routine… all the unglamorous things that actually make you feel strong enough to hold what you’re calling in. Staying present with yourself does more for your manifestation than doom scrolling or getting lost in parasocial dynamics. Your energy is your power source. Protect it.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, everything comes into focus through your relationship sector, but in a way that feels different from the past few years. Uranus is no longer in Taurus shaking things up, so there’s a sense of steadiness that may feel unfamiliar at first. You’re not guessing where you stand or overanalyzing mixed signals. You’re building from a place of clarity, whether that’s with a partner or within yourself as you define what you’re available for. This is a moment to set intentions around the kind of love that actually feels safe, grounded, and mutual. And over the next six months, you’ll start to see how the boundaries you set now, the standards you hold, and the honesty you practice shape a much healthier version of connection than you’ve experienced in a long time.

Sagittarius Sun & Rising:

Sag, the week begins with your attention close to home, even if your mind is still halfway somewhere else. With the moon in Pisces activating your home, family, and emotional foundation, you may feel more nostalgic, reflective, or simply in the mood to slow down and be in your own space. It’s a softer kind of energy that lets you reconnect with yourself without needing to go anywhere. Then midweek, the moon enters Aries and your spark comes back fast. Suddenly you want to move, create, flirt, say yes to something fun, maybe even revisit an idea that felt too far-fetched a few days ago. There’s a playful courage that reminds you how easy it is for your energy to shift when you follow what excites you.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, the focus lands on your routines, your health, and the way your daily life is actually structured. But this isn’t about strict discipline or forcing yourself into something rigid. There’s something satisfying about creating rhythms that feel good to live in: meals you enjoy, movement that feels natural, a schedule that leaves room for both productivity and pleasure. This week’s new moon invites you to build a lifestyle that supports your energy instead of draining it. The small choices you make now, especially the ones that feel sustainable, start shaping a version of your life that feels a lot better to wake up to over the next six months.

Capricorn Sun & Rising:

Capricorn, the week begins with your mind moving in a softer, more curious direction. With the moon in Pisces activating your communication sector, conversations feel more intuitive and less transactional. You might find yourself lingering a little longer in exchanges, noticing tone, subtext, the way something feels rather than just what’s being said. Then midweek, the moon enters Aries and pulls your focus inward toward home and personal matters. You may feel the urge to reset your space, tend to family, or simply carve out time where you don’t have to be “on” for anyone else. That shift helps you come back to yourself.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, the energy opens up into something much more enjoyable. This lands in your sector of creativity, romance, and pleasure, and there’s a noticeable ease to it. After everything you’ve been holding together, this feels like a reminder that life’s meant to be lived, not just managed. You might feel drawn to create, to flirt, to express yourself in ways that feel natural and unforced. There’s something very satisfying about choosing joy without overthinking it. The intentions you set now, especially around what you want to experience more of, begin shaping a version of your life that feels richer, warmer, and more aligned over the next six months.

Aquarius Sun & Rising:

Aquarius, the week begins with you focused on what actually supports you. With the moon in Pisces activating your money and self-worth sector, you may feel more reflective about your resources, your habits, and the way you value your time and energy. You’re in tune with what feels secure, what feels draining, and what feels worth your time and energy. Then midweek, as the moon enters Aries, your pace picks up. Brilliant ideas come in waves, and you might feel the urge to act on a project you can’t stop thinking about. The Aries Moon encourages you to make a shift from internal awareness to external movement, and it helps you bring your thoughts into real-world action.

By the time the Taurus New Moon arrives on the 16th, your focus turns toward your home and your sense of grounding. This is a more comforting energy than you’ve been used to, and it lands in a way that feels surprisingly good. You might feel inspired to create a space that reflects who you are now, to reconnect with family or chosen family, or simply to slow down enough to enjoy where you are. This lunation wants you to indulge in the pleasures of good food, a calm environment, and a sense of ease in your surroundings. The intentions you set now begin shaping a foundation that feels more stable and supportive over the next six months.

Pisces Sun & Rising:

Pisces, the week begins with the moon in your sign, so everything feels a little louder… your emotions, intuition, and your imagination. You’re picking up on everything, dreaming more vividly, feeling more connected to your ideals, and maybe even romanticizing a few things along the way. It’s a beautiful place to start the week, as long as you don’t lose yourself in it. Then midweek, the moon moves into Aries and brings you back into your body. Suddenly it’s about what’s real, what’s tangible, what you actually need. Money, energy, time… you’re more aware of what you’re giving and what you’re getting, and that awareness helps you recalibrate without overthinking it.

By the time the Taurus New Moon strikes on the 16th, your voice comes into focus in a way that feels both grounded and a little indulgent. This lands in your communication sector, making you act in a way that’s more intentional, sensual, and even a bit flirtatious. You’re carefully choosing your words, your tone, and your timing, and people can feel the difference. There’s pleasure in expressing yourself clearly and in saying what you mean without diluting it. The intentions you set now around communication, connection, and curating your immediate environment begin shaping a rhythm in your life that feels smoother, more aligned, and a lot more satisfying over the next six months.
